description: Forge — local self-maintenance Matrix instance (Session 34 brain swap → Session 36 Phase 3 tooling expansion). Powered by Claude Opus 4.7 + GPT-5.5 via opencode.ai/zen with the identity preamble injected at every Decode + Stream call. MCP fleet rooted at /root/matrix: fs (file ops), git (version control), fetch (external HTTP), forge-bridge (shell.exec + opencode.run sub-agent for free-form tasks). The cortex/store, knowledge, and journal subdirectories are denied at the daemon HTTP layer (ForgeFSPolicy) per matrix.kvx sess#34 Q3=c — replay byte-identity (cortex §13.4) + kvx ledger integrity + envelope chain audit log are non-negotiable; everything else is fair game for self-modification, recoverable via git history. PackageDigest values are PLACEHOLDERS — operators MUST replace with sha256 of the installed package before any production-anchored deployment.

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read a file (deprecated alias of read_text_file)

tools.name: read_text_file
tools.description: Read the contents of a text file

tools.name: read_media_file
tools.description: Read an image or audio file as base64

tools.name: read_multiple_files
tools.description: Read multiple files in a single call

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Write content to a file (creates or overwrites). The fs MCP server enforces no writes under /root/matrix/cortex/store, /root/matrix/knowledge, or /root/matrix/journal via its allowedDirectories arg; the daemon ALSO refuses these paths via ForgeFSPolicy if the agent attempts an out-of-scope HTTP call.

tools.name: edit_file
tools.description: Apply line-based edits to a file (same allow/deny enforcement as write_file)

tools.name: create_directory
tools.description: Create a directory tree (same allow/deny enforcement)

tools.name: list_directory
tools.description: List entries in a directory

tools.name: list_directory_with_sizes
tools.description: List entries in a directory including byte sizes

tools.name: list_allowed_directories
tools.description: List the directories the server is permitted to access

tools.name: directory_tree
tools.description: Recursive tree view of a directory

tools.name: move_file
tools.description: Move or rename a file (same allow/deny enforcement)

tools.name: search_files
tools.description: Recursive glob search

tools.name: get_file_info
tools.description: Stat a file or directory

tools.name: fetch
tools.description: Fetch a URL and return its content as Markdown. Useful when the agent needs to look up Go stdlib docs, Anthropic API references, or other external context while self-maintaining.

tools.name: git_status
tools.description: Show the working-tree status of /root/matrix

tools.name: git_diff_unstaged
tools.description: Diff unstaged changes (Forge's primary review surface — every self-edit shows up here first)

tools.name: git_diff_staged
tools.description: Diff staged changes

tools.name: git_diff
tools.description: Diff between commits/branches

tools.name: git_log
tools.description: Show commit history (the load-bearing audit trail for self-maintenance work)

tools.name: git_show
tools.description: Show a specific commit

tools.name: git_branch
tools.description: List branches

tools.name: git_add
tools.description: Stage files for commit

tools.name: git_reset
tools.description: Reset staged changes

tools.name: git_commit
tools.description: Create a commit from staged changes. Forge commits to feature branches; merge to main is human-gated through the diff viewer (Phase 3).

tools.name: git_create_branch
tools.description: Create a new branch (Forge defaults to forge/<intent_id> per self-maintenance task)

tools.name: git_checkout
tools.description: Switch branches

tools.name: shell_exec
tools.description: Run a shell command via /bin/bash -lc inside /root/matrix (cwd-jailed). PATH locked to system bins; MATRIX_DAEMON_TOKEN scrubbed from child env. Output capped at MATRIX_FORGE_SHELL_MAX_BYTES (default 256 KiB) so a runaway `cat huge.bin` doesn't blow the LLM context. Use this for `go test`, `go vet`, `go build`, npm scripts, etc. — anything that needs more than a typed git verb.

tools.name: opencode_run
tools.description: Spawn `opencode run "<prompt>"` and return the final assistant message. Same brain Matrix already routes through (Claude Opus 4.7 + GPT-5.5 via opencode.ai/zen) but as a sub-agent for free-form tasks that don't fit Matrix's IR (e.g. ad-hoc code review, brainstorming a refactor, drafting a doc). Use sparingly: it's slow + spends opencode credits + is the agent talking to itself by another path.
